bottleneck.title: When architecture becomes the bottleneck
bottleneck.intro: Engineering leaders usually reach me when:
items: The system can't scale without adding more infrastructure
Cloud costs keep rising faster than usage
Services are tightly coupled and fragile
Architecture decisions were never made explicitly
bottleneck.close: At this stage, the problem is rarely technology. It's architecture structure.

whatIDo.title: Architectural diagnostics and redesign for distributed systems
whatIDo.body: I analyze how your system is structured, where coupling lives and what blocks scalability — then define a clear path to evolve architecture safely.
whatIDo.close: The goal is predictable evolution — not just short-term fixes.

distributed.title: Distributed Systems & Event-Driven Architecture
distributed.description: Designing architectures where domains evolve independently, communication is decoupled and scalability emerges naturally.

cloud.title: Cloud Scalability & Architecture Modernization
cloud.description: Redesigning systems so infrastructure scales because architecture allows it — not to compensate for it.

dotnet.title: .NET Distributed Architecture
dotnet.description: Modernizing .NET systems toward modular, domain-aligned and cloud-ready architectures.

approach.title: Architecture before scale
approach.body: Scaling infrastructure without fixing architecture amplifies problems.
approach.close: So systems can grow without becoming fragile.

timeline.question: How long does a diagnostic take?
timeline.answer: Typically 1–2 weeks depending on system complexity and team availability.

access.question: What access do you need?
access.answer: Read access to repositories, architecture documentation (if available), and time with key engineers and stakeholders.

outputs.question: What format are the outputs?
outputs.answer: You receive a written report with diagrams, a recorded findings walkthrough, and ADR-style decision documentation.

pricing.question: How is pricing structured?
pricing.answer: The investment varies with the size and complexity of your system. The diagnostic exists so you know exactly what you're dealing with before any larger commitment. It starts with a no-cost conversation.

ctaBand.title: Ready to understand what's blocking your architecture?
ctaBand.body: Let's start with a conversation about your challenges.

hero.headline: Well-designed distributed systems don't stall. I help build that kind of system.
hero.intro: Distributed Systems Architect helping growing companies build scalable, maintainable systems.

story.title: Background
paragraphs: For over a decade, I've been designing and modernizing distributed systems for companies where scalability, reliability and evolution are critical.

I've worked with engineering teams across Brazil and the United States, helping them navigate complex architectural decisions — from migrating monoliths to microservices, to implementing event-driven patterns, to optimizing cloud infrastructure.
My focus is on systems that need to scale predictably without accumulating technical debt that slows down delivery.

beliefs.title: What I believe
items: Architecture is a decision system, not a diagram
Predictable scale comes from structural clarity, not more infrastructure
Domain boundaries should follow business boundaries
The best architecture is the one your team can evolve
Technical decisions should be explicit, documented and reversible

workWith.title: Who I work with
workWith.body: I typically work with CTOs, Heads of Engineering and Tech Leads at scale-ups and growing platforms who are facing architectural challenges that affect delivery, cost or reliability.
workWith.notFor: I don't work with early-stage startups without a structured operation, nor with companies looking only for one-off development or isolated bug fixes. My focus is on systems that already exist and need to scale — not on MVPs.
